goal: Migrate the wave 125 top-priority queue worker production path from direct restart/polling loops to the cancellable worker loop primitive.

selectedWorker.reason: Wave 125 selected the queue runner as the only production worker finding; this wave migrated both the runner lifecycle and the worker polling implementation reached by that runner.

before.runner: Queue runner owned a direct while(!stopped) bootstrap loop with local sleep(restartBackoffMs) after bootstrap errors.
before.worker: startQueueWorker owned a direct while(!stopped) polling loop with local sleep(pollIdleMs) for idle/error backoff and local sleep(compactionDelayMs) before processing claimed jobs.
before.api: startQueueWorker returned the existing stop() handle.
before.observability: Queue worker recorded worker_loop_failed and preserved queue.worker logs.

after.runner: Queue runner uses runCancellableWorkerLoop with AbortController shutdown, defaultWorkerLoopClock, same restartBackoffMs, same maxBootstrapRestarts, and same bootstrap error logs.
after.worker: startQueueWorker uses runCancellableWorkerLoop with AbortController shutdown, injectable WorkerLoopClock, same pollIdleMs idle/error backoff, same compactionDelayMs delay, and same queue processing dispatch.
after.observability: worker_loop_failed observability and queue.worker logs are preserved; transient claim failures continue after errorBackoffMs.

coverage: runner no unconditional production restart loop
runner uses cancellable loop primitive and abort shutdown
transient claim error backs off without spinning
worker loop primitive tests remain green
